title: castilleja ambigua subsp. humboldtiensis
intro: Castilleja ambigua subsp. humboldtiensis, also known as Yellow Indian Paintbrush, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States. This plant is known for its bright yellow and red flowers, which are shaped like a paintbrush. The medicinal properties of Castilleja ambigua subsp. humboldtiensis include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve coughs, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting respiratory health.
